events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u540d\u79f0: The prodigal son returns in Zhou Chu
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u7b80\u4ecb: The story of Zhou Chu's prodigal son turning back in Huang Sang's "Five Records of Jinling" is not entirely accurate.
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u5185\u5bb9: It wasn't until the 1980s, when I read Mr. Huang Sang's "Five Records of Jinling", that I realized that the stories about Zhou Chu I heard as a child were very inaccurate. Zhou Chu's father served as the governor of Poyang in Soochow when he was alive, but he died early. Therefore, the various rogue behaviors of Lord Zhou and the fear of his neighbors cannot be fully explained by his stubborn personality. Mr. Huang Shang had seen his portrait and had a very bad impression. He had the head of a leopard and the eyes of a leopard, looking like a complete gangster. This dear friend was later granted the title of Marquis. He first served as Dongguanling in Soochow, and was promoted to Wu Nan Governor and Taichang Qing. When Jin destroyed Wu, Zhou Chu, who had read poetry and books, was reborn again and became a minister of Jin. . When the prodigal son returned, he was no longer a teenager, but a middle-aged man in his 30s. But he did die heroically in the end, so the ancients lamented that he did not die in Eastern Wu and died in the Western Jin Dynasty, but it was a pity that Xiaohou Terrace was left in the south of the city. The Xiaohou Terrace mentioned here is the Zhou Chu Reading Terrace located at No. 44 Laohutou, Wuding Gate, Nanjing, commonly known as Zhou Chu Terrace.

events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u540d\u79f0: Jianchu Temple was founded
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u7b80\u4ecb: Sun Quan kept his promise and allowed the Kang Senghui to build a temple in Jiankang
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u5185\u5bb9: Jianchu Temple in the south of the city is one of the earliest strongholds of Buddhism in Nanjing. In the 10th year of Chiwu in the Eastern Wu Dynasty (247), when Kang Senghui set foot on this land, for Buddhism, this city was almost a virgin land, a new territory waiting to be opened up. The Kang Seng Hui, who had just arrived, dressed in strange clothes, called himself a Samana, and made exaggerated remarks, made people surprised, happy, and doubtful. In the early years, Sun Quan, the leader of Wu, came into contact with Zhi Qian, a monk who came to Wu to avoid chaos. He was shocked and only had a partial understanding of Buddhism. Kang Senghui stated the efficaciousness of Buddhism, boasted about the incomparable power of Buddha's bones and relics, and talked endlessly about the overhanging river. To Sun Quan, this was almost a fantasy. Empty talk is useless. Kang Senghui had no choice but to respectfully request him. Twenty-one days later, at the fifth watch, a loud sound suddenly came from the quiet night sky. Following the sound, he saw that a relic had fallen into the copper vase. This relic is indeed magical. The five-colored light illuminates the copper vase. It is also hard, like a diamond. It cannot be broken by an anvil. The copper plate will break immediately if it touches it. This extraordinary efficaciousness shocked the government and the public, and also frightened Sun Quan, the leader of Soochow. Sun Quan kept his promise and allowed the Kang Senghui to build a temple in Jiankang. This unprecedented temple was named Jianchu Temple, and the place where the temple was built was named Buddha Lane.
events.\u68c0\u7d22\u8bcd: Don't worry about changing your face

events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u540d\u79f0: Monk Kang Senghui visited Jianye, and Buddhism in Jiangnan began to flourish.
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u5185\u5bb9: Kang Seng, a monk from Kangju State, visited Jianye and set up statues to promote Buddhism. Sun Quan, the Emperor of Wu, praised the Buddha for his miraculous powers and built the Jianchu Temple and the Asoka Pagoda for him. This was the beginning of the rise of Buddhism in the south of the Yangtze River.

events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u540d\u79f0: Zhuge Liang Praises Stone City
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u7b80\u4ecb: During the Three Kingdoms period, Zhuge Liang was on an envoy to Eastern Wu. When he passed through Moling County, he admired its mountains and rivers like dragons and tigers, and suggested that Sun Quan move the capital here.
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u5185\u5bb9: It is said that during the Three Kingdoms period, Zhuge Liang went to Soochow as an envoy on the eve of the Battle of Chibi to discuss with Sun Quan the plan to defeat Cao Cao. It is said that when Zhuge Liang passed through Moling County, he rode to Stone Mountain to observe the mountain and river situation. He saw that the mountains headed by Zhongshan were winding and crouching in the southeast like a black dragon, while the mountains in the west, ending at Shitou Mountain, were like tigers on the banks of the river, so he uttered "Zhongshan Dragon Pan". "It is truly the residence of an emperor, surrounded by rocks and tigers," and suggested to Sun Quan that he move the capital to Moling.

events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u540d\u79f0: Sun Quan Established the Capital in Jianye
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u7b80\u4ecb: After the Battle of Chibi, Sun Quan moved the capital to Moling (today's Nanjing) and built the famous Stone City on Qingliang Mountain, making it a strategic military town.
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u5185\u5bb9: After the Battle of Chibi, Sun Quan moved to Moling (today's Nanjing) and renamed Moling Jianye. In the second year, the famous Stone City was built on the original city foundation of Qingliang Mountain. At that time, the Yangtze River flowed under Qingliang Mountain, so Stone City's military status was very prominent, and Sun Wu always used this place as the main navy base. In the following hundreds of years, this place became a strategically important military town. In the Civil War, the victory or defeat was often decided by capturing Stone City.

events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u540d\u79f0: Eastern Wu’s First Military Fortress
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u7b80\u4ecb: Eastern Wu founded the country with a navy and used Stone City as the headquarters of the navy. It became one of the largest docks along the Yangtze River at that time and were extremely important during the Six Dynasties era.
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u5185\u5bb9: The highest altitude of Gushitou Mountain is only over 60 meters above sea level. However, because before the Tang Dynasty the river approached the foot of the mountain and the Qinhuai River mouth was strangled to the south, its strategic position was very important. Sun Wu founded the country with a navy, and even used Stone City as the headquarters of the navy, making it one of the largest docks along the Yangtze River at that time. At that time, Sun Wu and later the Eastern Jin Dynasty and the Southern Dynasties often sent envoys to other countries in East Asia and Southeast Asia, leaving many records of friendly exchanges. Throughout the Six Dynasties, Stone City received special attention from the dynasty because it was located at a strategic location. The emperor often led the army to guard it with his trusted ministers. History says that once "there is a policeman on the riverside, he must first use the stone to defend himself."

events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u540d\u79f0: Gu Qiyuan Ji Jinling's "Five and Three Wonders"
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u7b80\u4ecb: Gu Qiyuan's "Visitor's Words" records the five and three unique features of Jinling
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u5185\u5bb9: Although there is some clue as to how this stele was circulated, Gu Qiyuan's "Guest Extravaganza" during the Wanli Period of the Ming Dynasty recorded the five kinds of three unique features of Jinling, which does not include this stele. Gu records the five kinds of three unique things: the five-body hand-made Buddha statues made by Daian Dao in the Song Dynasty at Waguan Temple, the one-body statue of Vimalakirti painted by Gu Changkang in the Jin Dynasty, and the jade Buddha presented by Shi Ziguo in Yixi Middle School in the Jin Dynasty, which is four feet and two inches high and has a clean jade color. It is moist, has a unique shape, and is almost unartificial. It is called the three unique things. Dong Yu of Qingliang Temple painted dragons, Li Houzhu wrote eight-point calligraphy, and Dong Xiaoyuan wrote cursive calligraphy, which are called the three unique things. The portraits of great officials painted by Zhang Sengyao of the Jin Dynasty in Linggu Temple were praised by Li Taibai, and Yan Lugong's "Book of Qing Officials" called them three masterpieces. We also studied Lu Guimeng's "Gu Jin Ji Yan" in Waguan Temple. In the temple, there is a sheep cart driven by Empress Chen, and a brocade skirt of Empress Zetian of the Tang Dynasty. A lecture hall was built in the Southern Tang Dynasty, and Wang Youjun's "Oath of Confession" was found in the bamboo cave with an owl kissing. This is the case. Waguan Temple should have three unique features. If we don't talk about the beauty, Mrs. Wu Zhao's machine, needle, and thread, all by one person, are especially the best.

events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u540d\u79f0: Zhu Yuanzhang burned the Gongchen Building on fire
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u7b80\u4ecb: Zhu Yuanzhang burned the Gongchen Building on fire
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u5185\u5bb9: There is a story about Zhu Yuanzhang burning down the Tower of Heroes. It says that after Zhu Yuanzhang became the emperor, he felt anxious every day. He thought that the founding heroes could help him ascend to the throne of the emperor. But if he changed his mind one day, wouldn't he be able to help others become the emperor? ! So he had a murderous intention and built a meritorious official tower near the Drum Tower, where he wanted to hold a banquet to celebrate the founding heroes. After the Meritorious Building was built, Liu Bowen accompanied Zhu Yuanzhang to inspect it and found that something was wrong. The walls of the building were made of copper, the pillars were hollow, and the windows upstairs were very small. Once the door of the building was locked from the outside, the people inside would There is no way to escape. On the day of the banquet, Liu Bowen excused himself from illness and quietly said to Xu Da, "You must be careful today. Wherever the emperor goes, you must follow him and never leave." Sure enough, after three drinks, Zhu Yuanzhang got up and left the table. Xu Da followed the emperor quietly. When Zhu Yuanzhang went out and saw Xu Da following him, he quickly asked him to go back and drink. Xu Da said: Your Majesty, won’t you even leave a Da? Zhu Yuanzhang knew that Xu Da had seen the flaw, so he had to leave in a hurry with Xu Da. Someone here had already locked the door of the Heroes Building and set the building on fire. When the heroes upstairs discovered that the walls and pillars of the building had been burned to a boil, they were all trapped inside and roasted to death.

events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u540d\u79f0: Nanjing Poetry Society
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u5185\u5bb9: During the orthodox period, there was a society in Jinling called Nanjing Poetry Society, whose members included Ni Qian and others. Volume 18 of "Collection of Ni Wenxi" "Preface to Yeting's Climbing Poems": "There are stories about climbing high in nine days. On the ninth day of the ninth month of every year, the gentlemen of the Nanjing Poetry Society would go to the Yuhuatai in the south of the city with wine and food, and they would be the masters of the poems. happy."

events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u540d\u79f0: Jiang Mingyu Association
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u5185\u5bb9: During the Wanli period, there was a society in Jinling called Jiang Mingyu Society, whose members included Pan Zhiheng, Jiang Mingyu, etc. Pan Zhiheng's "Biography of Zhu Wuxia": "Wuxia, courtesy name Taiyu... In Jiyou, Bing Huasheng formed a association with Qinhuai, and his reputation became more and more prosperous. First, Jiang Gongming came to his apartment, saw his "Leon Resentment Poems", and forced a visit to make friends with him. We began to recruit scholars to unite in Quzhong."

events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u540d\u79f0: Yecheng Grand Shrine
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u5185\u5bb9: In the thirty-seventh year of Wanli, there was a society in Jinling called Yecheng Grand Society, with more than 30 members including Yuan Zhongdao and Yu Xianchang. Volume 3 of Yuan Zhongdao's "You Ju Shi Lu": "Thirty scribes gathered at the Qinhuai Water Pavilion, each with their own thoughts. ...Moved into the river house at the Qinhuai Ferry, and went boating under the moon. More than thirty poets gathered at the Qinhuai River. Shui Pavilion, two girls’ school clerks, are Zhu Wuxia and Fu Lingxiu.”
events.\u76f8\u5173\u4eba\u7269: 袁中道、俞羡长、朱无瑕、傅灵修

events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u540d\u79f0: painting club
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u5185\u5bb9: During the Wanli period, there was a society in Jinling called the Painting Society, with Wang Wenyao among its members. Volume 1 of "Second Continuation of Jinling Trivial Affairs" "Painting Society": "Shaogang Wang Wenyaopu's paintings were outstanding in the Li family. He also did good things and collected many famous pens from the Song and Yuan Dynasties. Because he established a painting society in Qinhuai, all those invited to join the society were celebrities. .”

events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u540d\u79f0: Jinling Conference/First meeting of Guomen Guangye Society
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u5185\u5bb9: In the third year of Chongzhen's reign, there was a society in Jinling called Jinling Conference/Guomen Guangye Society's first gathering. Its members included Yao Huan and several thousand others. "Jingzhiju Poetry Talk": "We drank wine and sang songs, and held a gathering of "Fushe" colleagues on the Qinhuai River. Several thousand people gathered to write and write for the country."

events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u540d\u79f0: Annual meeting of the society
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u5185\u5bb9: In the third year of Chongzhen's reign, there was a society in Jinling called Fushe Tongnianhui, with Chen Zilong and others among its members. "Si Jiu Lu·Zhang Pu" says: "In Gengwu, we tried together in Nandu and met on the Qinhuai boat. They were all of the same year, Yang Weidou, Chen Wozi, Peng Yanyou, Wu Jungong, Wan Nianshao, Jiang Chuzhen, and Wu Laizhi. Many people have forgotten it.”
events.\u76f8\u5173\u4eba\u7269: 陈子龙、杨维斗、陈卧子、彭燕又、吴骏公、万年少、蒋楚珍、吴来之

events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u540d\u79f0: Lou Chuan Gathering
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u5185\u5bb9: In the ninth year of Chongzhen, there was a society in Jinling called Louchuan Yaji, with Yao Beiruo and others among its members. "Banqiao Miscellaneous Notes": "Yao Beiruo, Jiaxing, sailed to Qinhuai in a 12-story boat. He recruited more than a hundred well-known people from all over the world to take the exam, and invited four famous prostitutes to each boat to have a drink. A pear garden was lit with lights and music, which was a grand event. "

events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u540d\u79f0: Pan Zhiheng crossed Qinhuai three times
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u5185\u5bb9: From October to the first month of the following year, Pan Zhiheng stayed in Qinhuai for the winter three times. He hosted Gu's pavilion gatherings and performed more than a hundred performances with ladies and gentlemen. (Pan Zhiheng's "First Beauty")

events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u540d\u79f0: Wang Wenyao's Painting Society
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u5185\u5bb9: During the Wanli period, Wang Wenyao of the Shangyuan Dynasty established a painting society in Qinhuai, and many celebrities joined the society. (Volume 18 of "Jinling Tongzhuan")

events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u540d\u79f0: Mao Yuanyi launches Wuri Qinhuai Grand Shrine
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u5185\u5bb9: After that, Mao Yuanyi returned to An'an and entered Jinling for the first time. He built the Qinhuai Grand Shrine on the afternoon of the day and wrote the poem "Poem on the Day of the Day to Diao Miluo", which made him famous. (Volume 2 of "Inshu House Book Shadow")

events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u540d\u79f0: Gu Mengyou and dozens of others formed a Qinhuai society
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u5185\u5bb9: Gu Mengyou, Xing Mengzhen, Fang Erzhi, Tan Youxia and other dozens of people formed a society in Qinhuai. (Volume 82 of "Tongzhi Suzhou Prefecture", "Chronology of Mr. Xing Mengzhen", Song Mei's "Preface to Gu Yuzhi's Poems")
events.\u76f8\u5173\u4eba\u7269: 顾梦游、邢孟贞、方尔止、谭友夏

events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u540d\u79f0: Gu Mengyou and others formed the Qinhuai Society
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u5185\u5bb9: At the end of Chongzhen, Gu Mengyou and Wu Shide, Wang Huang, Xing Fang, Fang Wen, Ge Yilong, Shi Xuan, Yang Bu, Xue Gang, Tan Yuanchun and others tried to form a poetry club in Qinhuai, which was vigorous and popular. (Volume 82 of "Tongzhi Suzhou Prefecture", Song Mei's "Preface to Gu Yuzhi's Poems", "Chronology of Mr. Xing Mengzhen")
events.\u76f8\u5173\u4eba\u7269: 顾梦游、吴时德、王潢、邢昉、方文、葛一龙、史玄、杨补、薛冈、谭元春

events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u540d\u79f0: Pan Zhiheng Qingxi Club
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u5185\u5bb9: In the forty-seventh year of Wanli, there was a society in Jinling called Pan Zhiheng Qingxi Society. Its members included Pan Zhiheng, Tu Long, etc. Huang Juzhong's "Pan Beard and Weng Wuji's New Narratives": "Ji Wei came to Baimen and lived in Qingxi. He wrote "Qingxi Shecao"."

events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u540d\u79f0: star club
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u5185\u5bb9: In the third year of Chongzhen's reign, there was a society in Jinling called Xingshe, with nearly a hundred members including Wu Kuang. Volume 4 of "Biography of Fushe's Surnames": "Wu Kuan, whose courtesy name is Zhongxiang, lives in Weixiang in the south of the city. At the time of the ceremony, the literary society was in Tianjie Temple. Nearly a hundred people gathered together and wrote two poems. They stopped before noon."

events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u540d\u79f0: Yu Anqi and others joined Chunshe
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u5185\u5bb9: In the spring, Yu Anqi from Wuzhong settled in Sanshan, and formed a Chunshe Society with Xie Zhaozhe from Zhong Fujian. (Xie Zhaozhe's "Preface to Spring Society")

events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u540d\u79f0: "Stone City Auspicious Snow"
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u7b80\u4ecb: The painter Guo Ren painted "Eight Scenes of Jinling", and Qingliang Mountain was listed among them as "Snow in Stone City"

events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u540d\u79f0: Shogun Mountain is also called "Lime Mountain"
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u7b80\u4ecb: The ridges of Shogunate Mountain are rocky, and during the Ming Dynasty residents began to mine the rocks to make lime, so Shogunate Mountain is also called Lime Mountain.
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u5185\u5bb9: After the Ming Dynasty, with the increase in population and social and economic development, residents near Mufu Mountain gradually discovered that the ridges of Mufu Mountain were full of rocks, which could be made into lime by calcining them. So, driven by economic interests, they began to mine rocks on a large scale. Mufu Mountain It was also renamed Lime Mountain.

events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u540d\u79f0: Many temples were destroyed by fires
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u7b80\u4ecb: Gaozuo Temple declined in the Ming and Qing Dynasties.
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u5185\u5bb9: In the early years of Hongwu, the Gaozuo Temple was abolished and most of the buildings were destroyed by fire. Many buildings were rebuilt during the Hongli period, but declined in the late Qing Dynasty.

events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u540d\u79f0: The monument to Lu Yanzhi is built in the foundation laying room of Sun Yat-sen Mausoleum
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u7b80\u4ecb: The monument to Lu Yanzhi is built in the foundation laying room of Sun Yat-sen Mausoleum
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u5185\u5bb9: Lu Yanzhi, the designer of the Sun Yat-sen Mausoleum, loved painting since he was a child in Paris. He studied in the Department of Architecture of Tsinghua University and the Department of Architecture of Cornell University in the United States. As a student and assistant of the famous American architect Mauro Felipe, he participated in the formulation of the " "Capital Plan" provided a thorough and detailed plan for the overall planning of Nanjing's modern urban construction; he also participated in the architectural design work of Jinling Women's University and Yenching University, and showed excellence in exploring the integration of the essence of Chinese and Western architecture. talent. Later, he also designed the Sun Yat-sen Memorial Hall in Guangzhou. When presiding over the construction project of Sun Yat-sen Mausoleum, Lu Yanzhi was extremely serious and responsible, worked hard and showed a high degree of professionalism. However, he eventually became ill due to overwork and died at the age of 36, without being able to see the final completion of his brilliant work. In recognition of his outstanding contribution to the construction of Sun Yat-sen Mausoleum, in May 1930, the Cemetery Management Committee decided to build a monument to Lu Yanzhi in the foundation laying room of Sun Yat-sen Mausoleum. At the top of the monument is a bust of Lu Yanzhi, and the lower part is engraved with an inscription written by Youren: "Lu Yanzhi, the architect of the Prime Minister's Mausoleum who supervised the mausoleum workers, died of overwork. The Prime Minister's Cemetery Management Committee decided to erect a stone to commemorate it on May 28, 1999."

events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u540d\u79f0: Ji Gang visited Xuanwu Lake with friends
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u7b80\u4ecb: Ji Gang visited Xuanwu Lake with friends
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u5185\u5bb9: The day before the Double Ninth Festival of the next year, it was time to take a rest. Mr. Ji Gang and Mr. Wang Dong, Wang Yi, Wang Pijiang and Wu Mei went to Xuanwu Lake together. There are no restaurants on the lake, so Mr. Ji Gang made preparations in advance and "brought wine crabs with him". Mr. Zhu sat in the Zhoushan Tea House, "Looking at the mist of Zhongshan Mountain, the traces of smoke in the north, the withered lotus and yellow willows, and the desolate autumn face, I wrote a poem about it in a series of sentences. I came back under the moonlight and drank together again. We started to disperse at ten o'clock. . There are many Qu Andu songs. Today’s trip, I really appreciate the beauty of traveling and enjoying it." Wandering around the lakes and mountains, poetry and wine, drinking in this kind of situation is full of excitement and can best reveal your soul and talents.

events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u540d\u79f0: Bohan angrily threw the case into a stirrup
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u7b80\u4ecb: After being drunk for three days, Bohang "threw the stirrup on the table in anger"
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u5185\u5bb9: We talked about the name for a long time, because Mr. Bohan lives up to his name, and his name lives up to his name. Mr. Qi Yi, a young man, once studied "Shuowen Jiezi" from Mr. Gao Zian, a famous scholar in Jiangning, and learned all about it. Before Gao Zian died of illness, he relied on the manuscripts of his poems. He studied at Zhongshan Academy in Nanjing in his early years and excelled in his studies. He was deeply loved by Qichun Huang Xiangyun, the principal of the academy. After losing his crown, he indulged in drinking and often "drinked with friends and wrote poems. He wore brocade clothes and jade, jumped on his horse and gathered treasures outside the door. Those who saw him looked at him like a prince and grandson." Once, after being drunk for three days, he "thrown a stirrup on the table angrily" (Qian Kunxin ("A Narrative of Mr. Winter Drinking", the same below), and almost burned down the study. Mr. stopped drinking from then on.

events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u540d\u79f0: Qiu Fengjia has a deep connection with Nanjing
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u7b80\u4ecb: Qiu Fengjia's works praise the Revolution of 1911
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u5185\u5bb9: He has a deep connection with Nanjing during the Republic of China, and can even be called one of the founders of the Republic of China. After Guangdong became independent in late November 1911, he was elected as one of the representatives of Guangdong Province in the Provisional Assembly of the Republic of China in Nanjing, and he was also the only Taiwanese representative. During the Nanjing Conference, Qiu Fengjia was particularly delighted. He traveled in the snow to Ming Xiaoling Mausoleum, Mochou Lake and other places of interest. He composed ten poems in succession, including "Visiting Ming Xiaoling Mausoleum", "Climbing Sweeping Leaf Tower", and "Wandering to Mochou Lake in the Snow". Praising the Revolution of 1911 and expressing his joy, the poet looked inside the world, seeing vitality everywhere and national rejuvenation in sight, and he felt a lot of emotion.
events.\u76f8\u5173\u4f5c\u54c1: 《谒明孝陵》、《登扫叶楼》、《雪中游莫愁湖》

events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u540d\u79f0: Zhu Ziqing created works with Nanjing as the background, and traveled to the Qinhuai River with Yu Pingbo to create travel prose.
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u7b80\u4ecb: Zhu Ziqing visited Nanjing many times, and his prose "Nanjing" revealed Zhu Ziqing's praise of Nanjing. He traveled to the Qinhuai River with Yu Pingbo and wrote travel prose.
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u5185\u5bb9: Zhu Ziqing visited Nanjing many times and wrote the narrative prose "Back View" and the lyrical prose "Qinhuai River under the Shadow of Oars and Lanterns" and "Nanjing" with Nanjing as the background. The narrative prose "Back View" describes the experience of his father bidding farewell to the author at Nanjing Station in the winter of 1917. The characters and plot in the prose are very simple, and the writing is clear. It uses the technique of memory to record the father's back view on the station that was fixed in "my" heart. . "The Qinhuai River under the Sound of Oars and the Shadow of the Lamp" is a travel prose written by Zhu Ziqing and Yu Pingbo after they traveled to the Qinhuai River together in August 1923. Yu Pingbo also wrote a prose with this title at the same time. These two essays have become twins in the modern literary world. Zhu Ziqing's words succinctly describe the beginning and end of the tour, describing the lights under the roof of the seven-board cabin, the lights and mist on the boats shrouded in night, and walking along the bank of the Qinhuai River. The sound of string singing, the Dazhong Bridge and the dilapidated houses beside the bridge are all described in detail. Yu Pingbo's article intercepts the lights, lights, shadows, rivers and cruise ships outside Dazhong Bridge to make a rough outline. The work does not focus on describing events and scenery, but focuses on the inner philosophical thinking. In his journey through life, he considered the chaotic world and refused the invitation of the singer on the Qinhuai River. It is not out of moral considerations. His thoughts go far beyond the specific scene of the Qinhuai River and sublimate to the inescapability of suffering in life. All living beings suffer but love will last forever. The lyrical prose "Nanjing" was written by Zhu Ziqing in August 1934. In this essay, the author appreciates Nanjing with a glance, from ancient buildings to food culture, from ancient ruins to modern changes, and has a panoramic view of all aspects of the ancient city of Nanjing, which is naturally elegant and endearing. There is a touch of sadness and nostalgia in the article, but also infinite appreciation and praise.

events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u540d\u79f0: Pearl Buck settled in Nanjing, began literary creation, and won the Nobel Prize for Literature for her description of Chinese themes.
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u7b80\u4ecb: Pearl Buck created works and novels from both Chinese and Western perspectives to describe the situation of students, reflecting the confrontation between East and West, tradition and modernity. There is a broad market for novels with Chinese materials. She won the Nobel Prize for Literature for "The Good Earth" and built a bridge between Eastern and Western literature.
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u5185\u5bb9: From 1920 to 1935, Pearl Buck settled in Nanjing with her husband, and was employed at Jinling University and National Southeast University, teaching English and religious courses. The advantage of being proficient in Chinese allowed her to carefully observe the surrounding environment during this period and embark on the path of literary creation. As the daughter of American missionaries who was born and raised in China, she is able to adopt a dual-dimensional Chinese and Western perspective in her writing, observing and writing as a marginalized listener. In 1923, Pearl Buck published "Also in China" in "The Atlantic Monthly", describing some changes that had occurred in female students. In 1924, she published two articles, "Beauty in China" and "The Ideological State of Chinese Students", in "Forum" and "China", describing in detail the situation of the Chinese students around her. In 1925, Pearl Buck published a novel titled "A Chinese Woman Speaks" in "Asia", and then wrote a sequel to the novel. The two novels embody the confrontation between East and West, tradition and modernity. In April 1930, she published the two novels under the title "East Wind·West Wind", which was well received by readers. In 1930, Pearl Buck published "The Good Earth," an English novel describing rural life in China, which made her a huge success and helped her win the 1938 Nobel Prize for Literature. The novel reproduces the original rural life scenes in a realistic way. Pearl Buck is a transcendent individual for Nanjing literature. She was born in the United States and grew up in China. Chinese and English are both her native languages. She is not only the first female writer in the United States to win the Nobel Prize for Literature, but also the only American writer to win the Nobel Prize for literature for describing Chinese themes. Pearl Buck's "Writing on Chinese Themes" became a bridge between modern China and the Western world, allowing Westerners, especially Americans who were in economic crisis and ideological crisis at that time, to know more about China. This text enriched the understanding of modern Chinese literature in modern times. An observation of Chinese people’s survival and life.

events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u540d\u79f0: Wang Anshi petitioned to use Xuanwu Lake as farmland
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u7b80\u4ecb: Wang Anshi petitioned to use Xuanwu Lake as farmland
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u5185\u5bb9: Wang Anshi's "changes in nature are not powerful, ancestors are not lawful, and people's words are not compassionate" could still become the spiritual weapon of politicians until the middle of the 20th century; but his political achievements in Nanjing are probably not very easy to describe, among which the most The famous one was to petition the emperor to use Xuanwu Lake as farmland. As long as Xuanwu Lake still exists today, we can understand his absurdity. It is said that there was a political joke at that time, saying that Prime Minister Wang also planned to turn Taihu Lake into farmland, but he didn't know where to get the water from Taihu Lake. Someone reported that it would be easy, just dig another Taihu Lake and release the water there. As for the "New Meanings of the Three Classics" compiled by Wang Anshi in Nanjing, it seems that even later generations of Confucian scholars have not mentioned it. According to folklore, they were very dissatisfied with Wang Anshi's various "disturbing" measures in his reforms, and had a deep hatred for him. This can be seen by reading the article "Ao Xianggong drank and hated Banshan Hall" in "Warning to the World".

events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u540d\u79f0: Shao Bo slandered Wang Anshi
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u7b80\u4ecb: Shao Bo wrote "On Distinguishing Adultery" to slander Wang Anshi
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u5185\u5bb9: For example, Shao Bo not only wrote an article "Discrimination of traitors", slandering Wang Anshi as unreasonable, but also made up a story in "Shao's Record of Hearing and Seeing", saying that Wang Jinggong summoned an old soldier in Banshan Garden to help him draw a spring. Sweeping the floor, of course he would be full of praise when he was satisfied, but if he was slightly unsatisfied, he would get angry and drive him away. This is nothing more than portraying Wang Anshi as a moody villain and tarnishing his face.

events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u540d\u79f0: Wang Anshi’s relationship with Su Dongpo
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u7b80\u4ecb: Wang Anshi’s relationship with Su Dongpo
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u5185\u5bb9: The fact is that in July of the seventh year of Yuanfeng (1084), Su Dongpo returned from Huangzhou to the north and passed through Jinling, where he stayed for three months. Accompanied by Wang Shengzhi, the prefect of Jiangning, Su Dongpo made a special trip to Banshan Garden to visit Wang Anshi and visit Zhongshan together. Wang and Su both came from Ouyang Xiu's family. During the Xining period, they served as officials in the capital, but they did not have much contact with each other. This time, the two literary masters finally had the opportunity to get in close contact. They stayed together for more than a month, talking, traveling, chanting, and singing, and deepened their understanding of each other. As time passes, it seems like two skies, which have just experienced the baptism of the storm, are particularly clear and calm. These two great minds instantly cleared up all their previous grudges and understood each other. Su Dongpo admired Wang Anshi very much. After reading "Guizhixiang·Jinling Nostalgia", he sighed: "This old man is just a wild fox spirit." After leaving Jiangning, Su Dongpo crossed the river to Yizheng and sent a poem about Jiang Shan's travels. . After Wang Anshi read it, he was in awe, and especially praised two lines in it: "There are so many peaks that cleverly block the sun, and the river is far away and it seems to be floating in the sky." He sighed: "I have never written poems like these two lines in my life."

Su Dongpo's "Four Poems of Cijing Gongyun", one of which is related to Wang Anshi's "Beishan", mentions this incident: Riding a donkey into the wilderness, I want to see my husband when he is not sick. He advised me to try to get a three-acre house. It has been ten years since I joined the father-in-law.

events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u540d\u79f0: Wang Anshi visits Zanyuan
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u7b80\u4ecb: Wang Anshi visited Zan Yuan, and the two sat in silence facing each other.
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u5185\u5bb9: When Wang Anshi lived in Dinglin Temple in his later years, he felt a little irritable, so he went to visit Zan Yuan. The two sat in silence facing each other, which is what Wang Wei's poem said, "Anzai Zen restrains the poisonous dragon". "In my old age, I also live alone, and I am surprised every time there is a carriage or horse." This is what he said, but he also said, "Yao Jie's right and wrong are still in a dream, because he knows that he has not completely forgotten the rest of his habits."

events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u540d\u79f0: Qinhuai Eight Beauties
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u7b80\u4ecb: The eight beauties of Qinhuai became a disaster
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u5185\u5bb9: In the generation of the Eight Beauties of Qinhuai, there was an unprecedented breakthrough. The trouble was no longer the charming concubines in the palace, who were good at power and chaos, but the prostitutes who roamed the market. The most controversial person is undoubtedly Chen Yuanyuan. Wumei Village, which is known as the history of poetry, also wrote the famous line "A woman becomes a beauty in anger". The key to changing dynasties seems to be all tied to the fragrant shoulders of a prostitute! Until Ding Chuanjing of the late Qing Dynasty wrote "The Vicissitudes of Life", he still held this view unchanged. The reputation of a scholar was also lost to beauty. Gong Dingzi, who became the second minister, shamelessly blamed Gu Hengbo for the Qing Dynasty: I wanted to die for my integrity, but my concubine refused! Dong Xiaowan was even more mysterious. He was able to confuse the new Shunzhi Emperor into giving up his throne and becoming a monk in Mount Wutai.

events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u540d\u79f0: Yuan Mei was kicked out by Liu Yong
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u7b80\u4ecb: Yuan Mei was kicked out by Liu Yong
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u5185\u5bb9: When Liu Yong, who came from a prime minister's family and was also a famous calligrapher in the Qing Dynasty, took office as the governor of Jiangsu, he may have heard the rumors mentioned above, and he was very angry that Yuan Mei did not take the initiative to visit him, so he was ready to order Yuan Mei to be kicked out. Suiyuan. In a poem written to his brother Yuan Shu, Yuan Mei said: "I have wasted twenty frosts in the white sky, and I am just worried about returning home. Sure enough, it is my blessing to drive away guests, so that the West Lake is in my hometown." This is of course a helpless and depressed song. Just singing a high note. However, Yuan Mei later accepted the invitation and carefully wrote a "Jiangnan Enke Expression of Thanks" for Liu Yong, and the relationship between the two reconciled. When Liu Yong left Jiangsu, Yuan Mei wrote a long poem to see him off and sang his praises.

events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u540d\u79f0: Yuan Mei buried her father in the garden
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u7b80\u4ecb: Yuan Mei buried her father in Suiyuan and determined to live in Suiyuan until her death.
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u5185\u5bb9: At the age of 54, he obtained the consent of his mother and family and decided to bury his father in Suiyuan, which showed that he was finally determined to die in Suiyuan. "I will take the West Lake and move it to Jinling to see it."

events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u540d\u79f0: Zeng Guoquan was good at accepting power
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u7b80\u4ecb: Zeng Guoquan had the power to accept bribes. In order to avoid being noticed, the bribes were always transferred through an intermediary, and the place of contact was always the Diaoyu Lane brothel.
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u5185\u5bb9: It is said that after Zeng Guoquan became the governor of Liangjiang, he was good at taking bribes. In order to avoid being noticed, the bribes were always transferred through an intermediary, and the place of contact was always at the Diaoyu Lane brothel, which can be said to be "both elegant and popular". At that time, the Yamen of the Governor-General of Liangjiang had two east and west gates, with the four characters "Liangjiang Guarantee" and "Three Provinces Junheng" written on them respectively. Some people cleverly split the word "heng" into the two characters "鱼" and "行" by using the character splitting method, and the two characters "jun" and "diao" are only one "dot" apart, so "junheng" in three provinces becomes "junheng" "Three Provinces Fishing Bank", insinuating that Zeng Guoquan is the general owner of this "fishing (profit making) bank".

events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u540d\u79f0: Sun Quan established the capital in Jianye
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u7b80\u4ecb: Sun Quan moved the capital to Moling and changed its name to Jianye
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u5185\u5bb9: In 211 AD (the 16th year of Jian'an), Sun Quan moved his political center from Jingkou (today's Zhenjiang, Jiangsu) to Moling. The next year, he built the famous Stone City on the bank of the Qinhuai River in the west of today's Nanjing. Moling was renamed Jianye to express his determination to make contributions here.

events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u540d\u79f0: Point to the mountain as a barrier
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u7b80\u4ecb: Director Wang pointed out that the mountain is a fortress
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u5185\u5bb9: When the Eastern Jin Dynasty first established its foothold in Jiangzuo, it was full of waste. It is said that Wang Dao once pointed to Niutou Mountain, which is opposite Xuanyang Gate, the south gate of Jiankang capital, and said to Emperor Yuan of Jin Dynasty: This is a natural stone tower. At that time, the city was in dilapidated condition, and Director Wang pointed out that the mountain was a barrier, probably with the intention of comforting the emperor.

events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u540d\u79f0: Wang Huzhi refused to receive rice from the county magistrate
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u7b80\u4ecb: Wang Huzhi refused to receive rice from the county magistrate and preferred to go to Xie's house to beg for food.
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u5185\u5bb9: When Wang Huzhi lived in seclusion in Dongshan, he was so poor that he could hardly afford to eat. Tao Fan was the magistrate of Wucheng County at the time and brought a boatload of rice to him. Wang Huzhi resolutely refused. He proudly said that even if he was too hungry, he would only go to Xie's house to beg for food. It was not the turn of common people like Tao Fan to worry about him.

events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u540d\u79f0: Zhu Fatai's Lectures
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u7b80\u4ecb: Zhu Fatai lectures on the sutras. Every time he lectures, ladies and gentlemen from near and far, old and young, come in groups to listen.
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u5185\u5bb9: Zhu Fatai had profound Buddhist cultivation. Emperor Wen of Jin Dynasty respected him and invited him to give lectures. He also came to listen to the lectures in person. Princes, princes and ministers also flocked to him. Zhu Fatai's reputation grew bigger and bigger. Every time he gave a lecture, ladies and gentlemen from far and near, old and young, came in groups to listen. The scene was spectacular, and Waguan Temple became famous for a while.

events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u540d\u79f0: Tan belly east bed
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u7b80\u4ecb: The origin of the word "tanfudong bed"
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u5185\u5bb9: Taiwei Xi Jian had a beloved daughter who wanted to marry into the family of Prime Minister Wang Dao. After Director Wang agreed, he asked his disciples to go to the east wing of the palace in Wuyi Lane to apply for the title. It was hot summer at that time, and the Wang family's children were all neatly dressed and standing in the east chamber. The envoy sent by Xi Jian reported to Xi Jian after the blind date: The Wang family's nephews are all good. They are all well-dressed and elegant. They are standing respectfully in the east chamber as candidates. Only one person is sitting on the east bed wearing clothes. , with his belly exposed, eating alone, not caring about the important matter of this choice. Xi Jian listened and nodded and said with a smile: Judging from the behavior of this son, he is different from the popular customs, and he is exactly my ideal 'brother-in-law'! When he inquired, he found out that it was Wang Xizhi, so he married his daughter to him. This is the origin of the idiom "confidence in the east bed".

events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u540d\u79f0: Wang Dao built the shogunate here
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u7b80\u4ecb: After Emperor Jin and Yuan crossed the river, Prime Minister Wang Dao built the shogunate here, hence the name.

events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u540d\u79f0: Origin of the name "Kozaoji Temple"
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u7b80\u4ecb: Ganlu Temple was called Gaozao Temple because of the sermons given by "Taoist Gaozao".
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u5185\u5bb9: Ganlu Temple was built during the Yongjia Period of the Western Jin Dynasty and was named after the Ganlu Well built here during the Western Jin Dynasty. Bosilimitara gave lectures at Ganlu Temple and was called the "Gaozuo Taoist". Ganlu Temple was therefore commonly known as Gaozuo Temple.

events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u540d\u79f0: Wang Xianzhi and peach leaves
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u7b80\u4ecb: Wang Xianzhi's beloved concubine "Taoye" often traveled between the Qinhuai and Huaihe Rivers. Wang Xianzhi personally greeted her at the ferry and composed "Peach Leaf Song". Therefore, Nanpu Ferry is also called Taoye Ferry.
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u5185\u5bb9: Legend has it that Wang Xianzhi, a calligrapher of the Eastern Jin Dynasty, had a concubine named "Taoye". When she traveled between the Qinhuai and Huaihe Rivers, Wang Xianzhi was worried about her. He often greeted her at the ferry in person, and wrote "Peach Leaf Song" for her: "Peach leaves return to peach leaves. There is no need for a river; but there is no pain in crossing the river. I will welcome you." From then on, the ferry became famous and over time, Nanpu Ferry was also called Taoye Ferry.

events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u540d\u79f0: Praying for rain in the temple of Emperor Wu and Chiang of Liang Dynasty
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u7b80\u4ecb: Emperor Wu of Liang prayed for rain and paid homage to King Jiang’s temple
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u5185\u5bb9: In the sixth year of Tianjian (507), there was a severe drought in the capital, and he was ordered to pray for rain at Emperor Chiang's temple. But after a hundred days, there was still no movement. Emperor Wu of Liang was so angry that he wanted to burn down the temple. At the moment when the fire was about to be lit, Emperor Chiang appeared. Dark clouds covered the sky and rain fell. Emperor Wu of Liang hurriedly called for a stop. Originally, the emperor had not been to Chiang Kai-shek's temple once since he came to the throne, so he prepared a royal tour and brought his courtiers to pay homage. He sang and played loudly, moved the earth with his encouragement, and gave God Chiang face.

events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u540d\u79f0: Construction of Qixia Jingshe
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u7b80\u4ecb: When Monk Shao arrived in the Ming Dynasty, the number of people in Sheshan gradually increased.
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u5185\u5bb9: When Ming Sengshao arrived, Sheshan was still very desolate. He cut through the thorns, cut weeds, dredged ditches, built a monastery, and resumed teaching, which soon aroused a strong response in the most beautiful area. During the Southern Dynasties, Sheshan was densely forested and deep, and tigers often appeared. After Ming Sengshao came, the number of people near Sheshan gradually increased, and the tigers fled away. In the narrative of the inscription, this was exaggerated as a miracle that the Emperor Mingzheng did not disobey things and treated all kinds of benevolence with kindness. Of course, it can also be promoted as the victory of Buddhism.

events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u540d\u79f0: Emperor Wu of Liang Dynasty made Buddha statues in Qixia Temple
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u7b80\u4ecb: Emperor Wu of Liang Dynasty made Buddha statues in Qixia Temple
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u5185\u5bb9: Qixia Temple's famous Thousand Buddha Rocks began to attract attention. In the Liang Dynasty, Emperor Wu of the Liang Dynasty took the lead in worshiping Buddha. The Buddhist culture was particularly strong near Jiankang. Jiming Temple was only a short distance away from the palace gate. The first to get the moon near the water towers benefited the most. Qixia Temple is also a hotspot among Buddhist temples. When Xiao Hong, the king of Linchuan, was appointed as the governor of Yangzhou, he "used the heavenly prison for fifteen years to build a statue of Infinite Life, with the ground connected to the light and a total height of five feet." It became the most magnificent sculpture in the temple and still exists today. After the Liang Dynasty, new sculptures were built one after another, and the caves carved by the previous generation contained inscriptions by later generations. Thousand Buddha Rock has become the most concentrated, dense and important group of Buddhist sculptures in the Jiangnan area.

events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u540d\u79f0: Empress Chen stayed overnight at Qixia Temple
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u7b80\u4ecb: Empress Chen visited Qixia Temple twice to meet Master Huibu, and left an early chant of Qixia Mountain.
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u5185\u5bb9: In the first year of Zhenming (587) and the second year of Ming Dynasty (588), Empress Chen and his servants She Jiang, Guozi Jiujiu Xu Xiaoke and others went to the mountain twice to see Master Huibu and stayed overnight in the mountain house of Qixia Temple. I wrote some of the earliest chants about Qixia Mountain. Mr. Jiang also wrote the famous "Stele of Qixia Temple in Photography Mountain".

events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u540d\u79f0: Emperor Wu of Liang Dynasty made Buddha statues in Qixia Temple
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u7b80\u4ecb: Emperor Wu of Liang Dynasty made Buddha statues in Qixia Temple

events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u540d\u79f0: The legend of Mochou Lake
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u7b80\u4ecb: Mochou had no choice but to escape alone by boat. When she was leaving, she couldn't leave her folks behind. As the boat was circling around, a bank was built around it, which became today's Mochou Lake.
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u5185\u5bb9: There is a legend that Mochou was born between the Song and Qi Dynasties and was a poor girl from Luoyang. When she was fifteen, her father died of illness. In order to bury her father, she sold herself to the family of Yuan Lu in Shicheng and became her daughter-in-law. After the marriage, she gave birth to a son, Ahou. Later, my husband joined the army and guarded the border. We were separated for ten years and there was no news from him. Mo Chou devoted himself to helping the poor and the weak, and was deeply loved by his neighbors. Her husband's father-in-law objected strongly and even framed and humiliated her. Mo Chou couldn't bear the humiliation and threw himself into the city lake to death. In order to commemorate her, people renamed the lake Mochou Lake. Another recent legend is more tortuous. Mochou's real name is Qiu Nu, because she was born in Luoyang in autumn. After her mother died, she relied on her father to collect medicines and treat diseases, and the father and daughter depended on each other. Mochou followed her father up the mountain to collect herbs and tulips. Unfortunately, her father slipped and fell off a cliff and died. Qiunv felt miserable and cried on the street. She was so sad that she changed her name to Chounv. She was sold to bury her father, but was bought by Jiankang businessman Lu Yuanwai, who brought her back to Jiankang and made her his daughter-in-law. After marriage, the couple fell in love and had a son. The Lu family also built a Yujin Hall for her. Her husband changed her name to Mochou. One day, Emperor Wu of Liang came to Lu Yuanwai's home, saw Mochou, and planned to seize it for himself. He planned to recruit her husband to join the army, then killed Lu Yuanwai, and then summoned Mochou into the palace. Mochou had no choice but to escape alone by boat. When she was leaving, she couldn't leave her countrymen behind. As the boat hovered and circled, a bank was built around it, which became today's Mochou Lake. A villager lied to Emperor Wu of Liang that Mochou had jumped into the river and died. He felt guilty and wrote "Song of Water in the River".

events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u540d\u79f0: Zhu Daosheng enlightened
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u7b80\u4ecb: Zhu Daosheng attained enlightenment, and the large book "Nirvana Sutra" was transmitted to Jinling, which coincided with what Zhu Daosheng preached.
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u5185\u5bb9: Zhu Daosheng came from the north and stood out for his talent and spiritual enlightenment in his early years. He lived in seclusion in Lushan Mountain for seven years. Later, he traveled to Chang'an to study with Hui Rui, Hui Yan and others, and received training from Kumarajiva. After arriving in Jiankang, he stayed at Qingyuan Temple and silently studied Buddhism. One day, Zhu Daosheng, who was meditating deeply, suddenly realized that all living beings have Buddha nature and can achieve enlightenment and become a Buddha. He was so excited that he preached this idea everywhere, which shocked both the Taoist and secular circles of Jiankang City. Those conservatives who stick to the old views regard it as heresy and a scourge, and they attack it in groups. Daosheng was unmoved and insisted on his own theory. Sometimes he would not hesitate to swear to show his piety. Many years later, the large volume of the "Nirvana Sutra" reached Jinling, and it coincided with what Zhu Daosheng preached. All living beings lamented that Zhu Daosheng was so lonely and enlightened, and admired him with admiration. Zhu Daosheng held this sutra and ascended to the throne to preach. At that time, nature became more confident. Unfortunately, he had already left Qingyuan Temple by that time.

events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u540d\u79f0: Wang Bin debates Hui Chao
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u7b80\u4ecb: Wang Bin's brilliant argument and his sharp wit shocked everyone.
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u5185\u5bb9: Waguan Temple was the activity center of Taoism and secular circles in the Six Dynasties. Famous monks and scholars gathered here to engage in ideological exchanges. According to "The Biography of Lu Jue in Southern History", Wang Bin, a monk who later returned to secular life, went to Waguan Temple to listen to Master Yun's lecture on Chengshi Lun. The audience in the temple was packed with people, and all the seats were packed. Only the monk Zheng Huichao had an empty seat next to him. Wang Bin sat down. Hui Chao saw that he was rude and scolded him sharply. Wang Bin fought back and started a debate with Hui Chao. Wang Bin suddenly became a celebrity in Jiankang City. In "Shishuoxinyu", we can also see Liu Tan, Wang Meng, Huan Yi and other famous people gathering in Waguan Temple to discuss the figures of the Western Jin Dynasty and Jiangzuo. Discussions between celebrities and famous monks are often quick and witty confrontations.

events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u540d\u79f0: Treat Mr. Xiao like a stranger
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u7b80\u4ecb: Like a lady treating Mr. Xiao like a stranger
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u5185\u5bb9: It is said that there was a lady in a mansion in Jinling Paint Shop. She was originally a girl's school girl. She also flaunted her banners and fluttered flowers in Diaoyu Lane. Later, she was accepted as a star by the high officials and pampered in the special room. She was arrogant and extravagant. One day at a time. One day, Mrs. Ru was dressed in a bright dress and brought a group of servants to the Temple of Wealth to burn incense. The sound was so loud that it attracted people from the neighborhood to gather and watch. Mrs. Ru felt very proud. However, it was said that there was a scholar among the onlookers, who happened to be her old guest in the past, with whom she had a romantic relationship. Thinking of the old relationship, he strode forward and called Qingqing in a low voice, hoping to reminisce about the old good times and talk about his broad thoughts. Unexpectedly, the beauty turned back ruthlessly. She was so angry that she ordered her servants to escort the scholar back to the mansion. She also complained to the senior officials and ordered him to be sent to the county for severe punishment. It is certainly troublesome for a scholar to be sentimental. For example, it is enough for his wife to treat Mr. Xiao as a stranger, but it would be too much to treat him as an enemy and use his power to bully others.

events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u540d\u79f0: Liu Xie became a monk
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u7b80\u4ecb: Liu Xie was influenced by Emperor Wu's order to compile scriptures and vowed to become a monk.
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u5185\u5bb9: few decades later, Sengyou died. There were many Buddhist scriptures collected in his late period that needed to be sorted out. The person who was most familiar with these Buddhist scriptures at that time was Liu Xie. So Emperor Wu ordered Liu Xie to return to Dinglin Temple and sort out the scriptures with monk Huizhen. In the process of compiling Buddhist scriptures, Liu Xie believed in Buddhism and became more devoted. After the editing was completed, he applied to become a monk and burned his temples first, vowing to become a monk. With Emperor Wu's permission, Liu Xie took off his official uniform at Dinglin Temple, put on a monk's robe, changed his name to Huidi, and became a monk.

events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u540d\u79f0: The finishing touch
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u7b80\u4ecb: The source of the finishing touch
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u5185\u5bb9: "Records of Famous Paintings of the Past Dynasties" says that Zhang Sengyao painted four white dragons at Anle Temple in Jinling without dotting the eyes, saying: "If you dot the eyes, they will fly away." Everyone thought it was absurd and insisted on making some noise. As soon as Seng Yao clicked on the two dragons, thunder and lightning broke through the wall in a moment, and the two dragons flew up to the sky on the clouds. The two dragons who had not clicked on the eyes are now here.

events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u540d\u79f0: Emperor Wu was lucky enough to visit Qingxi Old Palace
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u5185\u5bb9: In the seventh month of autumn, Emperor Wu drove his car to the old palace in Qingxi. Music of gold and stone is played, and those in power compose poems.

events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u540d\u79f0: Master YunguangLecture on high platform
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u7b80\u4ecb: When Master Yunguang was giving lectures on the high platform of Ganlu Temple, flowers fell like rain, so the high platform was called Yuhuatai.
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u5185\u5bb9: In the early years of the Liang Dynasty, Zen Master Baozhi was the abbot of Ganlu Temple. According to legend, when Master Yunguang was lecturing on the high platform, flowers fell like rain, so the high platform was called Yuhuatai. In the Song Dynasty, it should be called Yongning Temple. In the Ming Dynasty, it was divided into two parts, with Yongning Temple in the east and Gaozuo Temple in the west.

events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u540d\u79f0: Li Gongzuo solves the case
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u7b80\u4ecb: Li Gongzuo solved the case and helped Xie Xiao'e find the murderer
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u5185\u5bb9: This is the spring of the eighth year of Yuanhe (813). Li Gongzuo, who had just been dismissed from his post in Jiangxi, felt relaxed. He went eastward along the Yangtze River, anchored his boat on the bank of the Qinhuai River, and stopped by Jinling for a few days to visit ancient times and find friends. In Waguan Temple where Waguan Pavilion is located, there was a monk named Qiwu who was a friend of Gongzuo. He told Gong Zuo a strange thing: Recently, a woman named Xie Xiao'e often came to the temple and asked for advice on how to explain it in two sentences. Xiao'e was from Nanchang, Jiangxi Province. She went out to do business with her father and husband. Unfortunately, she encountered bandits and both her father and husband died violently. She was injured by a robber and fell into the water. Fortunately, she was rescued. She begged along the way and came to Nanjing at that time, where she was taken in by the kind-hearted nun Jingwu of Miaoguo Temple. Xiao'e dreamed that her father said something to her: "Whoever kills me will be a monkey in a cart, and there will be grass in the door." Her husband also told her a dream and said: "Whoever kills me will walk in the grass and become a one-day husband." She believed it. The murderer's name was hidden in these two sentences, but after thinking hard, she still couldn't figure it out. She spent several years asking experts everywhere for advice, but she couldn't figure it out. After listening to Qi Wu's words, Li Gongzuo was very interested. He leaned against the threshold and began to think. It didn't take long for Li Gongzuo to figure it out. These are obviously two cryptic words, a riddle with split characters, and the two names Shen Lan and Shen Chun are hidden in them. "Monkey in the car" is the word "Shen": remove the upper and lower horizontal lines of the word "car", isn't it the word "Shen" in the middle? Among the twelve earthly branches, the one corresponding to the monkey is Shen. The three parts of Caotou, Men and Dong, taken together, are exactly the character LAN (traditional "LAN"). "Walking in the field" means passing through the "field", which is of course the word "Shen". The three parts of 1, day and husband combined form the word "spring". Li Gongzuo asked Qi Wu to call Xie Xiao'e over, and after asking for details face to face, he revealed the complete answer to the mystery he solved: Shen Lan and Shen Chun were the murderers of Xie Xiao'e's father and husband. Xie Xiao'e couldn't help crying, thanked her and left. Relying on this important clue provided by Li Gongzuo, Xie Xiao'e searched everywhere and finally found the murderer. Four years later, she captured and killed the thieves leaders Shen Lan and Shen Chun, and killed all their accomplices to avenge her relatives.

events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u540d\u79f0: The river moves westward
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u7b80\u4ecb: After the Tang Dynasty, the river gradually moved westward, and Stone City began to be abandoned and became a desolate and lonely "empty city".
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u5185\u5bb9: After the Tang Dynasty, the river gradually moved westward. After the eighth year of Wude (625) in the Tang Dynasty, Stone City began to be abandoned. Therefore, Liu Yuxi, a poet of the mid-Tang Dynasty, wrote a poem "Stone City": "The mountains surround the motherland, and the tide beats the empty city and returns lonely." . The old moon on the east side of Huai River came over the parapet wall late at night." The stone city described by the poet is already a desolate and lonely "empty city".

events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u540d\u79f0: "Stone City" by Liu Yuxi
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u7b80\u4ecb: "Stone City" by Liu Yuxi, a poet of the Tang Dynasty, depicts the desolate and lonely scene of Stone City, making "empty city" synonymous with Stone City.
events.\u4e8b\u4ef6\u5185\u5bb9: "The mountains surround the motherland, and the tide beats the empty city back lonely. In the old days on the east side of the Huai River, the moon comes late at night." This is a poem in Liu Yuxi's "Stone City", which makes "empty city" synonymous with Stone City. . However, this concept of "empty city" was originally a description of Jiankang City in the Tang Dynasty. The poet stood on a high place, with only the empty and lonely city walls and surrounding mountains in front of him. The high-rise building is majestic and desolate. The predecessors commented: "The mountains are there, the tide is there, and the moon is there, but the Six Kingdoms are not there, and the empty city ears also hurt the ancient times ("Tang Yin Gui Sign")."
